channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Trump’s Iran War Approaches a Fresh Legal Hurdle
item.description: Pressure will increase on congressional Republicans to vote to end the war if it surpasses the legal time limit.

item.title: An Economic War of Attrition
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have gone from lobbing missiles to inflicting pocketbook pain.

item.title: Lebanon Seeks to Extend Israel-Hezbollah Cease-Fire
item.description: But Hezbollah’s opposition to talks and continued Israeli strikes make progress difficult to come by.

item.title: Lebanon Is Done With Hezbollah
item.description: The talks between the neighboring states are about defeating their mutual enemy.

item.title: Iran Is Calling the Shots Now
item.description: Tehran is following Ho Chi Minh’s playbook in Vietnam.

item.title: Indonesia suggests charging a toll to transit the Malacca Strait
item.description: It’s not the first time that it has toyed with the unwelcome idea

item.title: Washington’s One-Dimensional Chess in the Horn of Africa
item.description: Trump’s inexplicable effort to lift sanctions on Eritrea reflects a deepening lack of strategy.

item.title: The Quad Is on the Brink of Extinction
item.description: It is hard to imagine the grouping enduring another two and a half years of Donald Trump.

item.title: Pete Hegseth Is America’s New Secretary of Pestilence
item.description: Reversing vaccine mandates is a disaster for military readiness.

item.title: Nepal’s Remittance Reckoning: The Gen Z Mandate Meets the Gulf Crisis
item.description: Kathmandu's new government was elected to right Nepal’s economic ship. It must now confront an external economic threat.

item.title: Wanted: a new finance writer
item.description: An opportunity to join the staff of The Economist

item.title: EU Aims Sanctions ‘Anti-circumvention Tool’ at Kyrgyzstan
item.description: The EU’s tool – branded as a “last resort measure” – is being used for the first time to cut off export of certain high-tech goods to Kyrgyzstan.

item.title: Africa Must Turn Climate Targets Into Green Strategies
item.description: African policymakers often take a “wish list” approach to their climate-action plans, without thinking about their investment potential or how they contribute to economic transformation. But a new framework can help them identify the most promising opportunities and match them with the right type of capital.

item.title: Spain’s China Bridge: Diplomacy or Dependency?
item.description: Securing a national win at the expense of a coherent EU strategy would be a self-defeating gamble for Spain.

item.title: After Trump, Partisanship Will Still Undermine U.S. Credibility
item.description: For foreign audiences, the dysfunctional nature of U.S. politics matters more than the country’s leader.

item.title: Breaking the Postwar Taboo: Japan Lifts Its Ban on Lethal Arms Exports
item.description: To fully understand its significance, we must situate the Cabinet decision within both its historical context and the evolving international environment.

item.title: China and Turkmenistan Inaugurate New Phase at Galkynysh Gas Field
item.description: Despite consistent chatter about the need for diversification, Ashgabat has struggled to reach customers other than China given infrastructure constraints.

item.title: Pakistan Is Mediating Between Iran and the US Because It Can – and It Must
item.description: Increased diplomatic clout made mediation possible, but the real motivation is the potential devastation the war could cause to Pakistan itself.

item.title: The high price of forever wars
item.description: Binyamin Netanyahu is quick to start conflicts, but shows no ability to end them

item.title: Pomp and pageantry won’t save Britain’s alliance with America
item.description: The special relationship? It’s complicated

item.title: Tim Cook wrote a winning recipe for Apple
item.description: Will it work for his successor?

item.title: Why Japan is loosening restrictions on exports of lethal arms
item.description: Takaichi Sanae, the hawkish new prime minister, hopes to spur a defence industrial renaissance

item.title: An anti-China protest lands Kazakhs in prison
item.description: Its economic power means China calls political shots, too

item.title: Europe’s defence startups face even bigger hurdles than America’s
item.description: They must contend with a fragmented market and hesitant customers

item.title: There is no better spur to military innovation than war
item.description: Defence firms in Ukraine make new weapons much faster and cheaper than most Western companies

item.title: Why Congress keeps getting dumber
item.description: Neither party has much tolerance for wily pragmatists like Thom Tillis

item.title: Wealthy New Yorkers grumble as a new tax looms over fortunes
item.description: Is the pied-à-terre tax a harbinger of things to come?

item.title: Artificial intelligence is creeping into American lawmaking
item.description: State legislators love using chatbots

item.title: Business
The weekly cartoon
Politics
Ukraine’s quest for new friends takes it to Turkey and Syria
item.description: American unreliability is creating conditions for new alliances of convenience

item.title: Donald Trump is giving psychedelic medicines a welcome boost
item.description: But his methods could prove damaging

item.title: Apple’s new boss needs to restore its magic for the AI era
item.description: The elevation of John Ternus hints at the future direction of the company

item.title: Jeff Bezos is raising his game in space
item.description: But it will be hard for Amazon and Blue Origin to close the gap with SpaceX

item.title: Abiy Ahmed is throttling free expression in Ethiopia
item.description: A leader who once championed press freedom now treats criticism as a crime

item.title: As the World Cup approaches, North American relations are at a nadir
item.description: President Donald Trump has wrecked the dream of a more united region

item.title: A botched election adds to Peru’s democratic dysfunction
item.description: A strongman’s daughter will face a leftist or an ultraconservative in the run-off

item.title: Wealthy New Yorkers grumble as a new tax looms
item.description: Is the pied-à-terre tax a harbinger of things to come?

item.title: China’s Naval Diplomacy Turns Back Toward Home
item.description: PLAN port visits within China itself are normalizing naval engagement at home and reinforcing political messaging.

item.title: Europe’s Economic Emergency
item.description: Rather than flit from one challenge to another, European leaders must get their priorities straight. Achieving stronger economic growth is essential because a robust industrial and technological base is a prerequisite for self-defense and strategic autonomy in an increasingly dangerous world.

item.title: A Test for Timor-Leste, and ASEAN, on Myanmar
item.description: Timor-Leste’s accession has created a rare opening for ASEAN to clarify whether it stands for anything more than procedural restraint.

item.title: Why Asia Feels the Hormuz Crisis Most
item.description: For Asia, the disruption to energy markets and global trade is immediate and consequential.

item.title: Policymakers vs. the Iran War Shock
item.description: Because markets, central banks, and governments move at different speeds—and sometimes in different directions—mistimed policy responses to major shocks can exacerbate, rather than mitigate, the depth and duration of a crisis. Fortunately, if this major lesson from the 1970s is heeded, the risk can be managed.

item.title: Honking is harming India’s health—and its economy
item.description: But nobody seems to care

item.title: Why Slums Persist
item.description: Slums persist not because they are desirable, but because they are necessary. The task for policymakers is not to eliminate slums by diktat, but rather to make them obsolete over time by promoting the educational opportunities and spatial integration their residents need.

item.title: America is vulnerable to electoral vandalism
item.description: Too many no longer believe elections are fair

item.title: Might Donald Trump try to rig the midterms?
item.description: We assess America’s democratic defences

item.title: Has the World Bank performed a U-turn on industrial policy?
item.description: Interventionists who think so should read its new report more closely

item.title: Renewables are shining. The Iran war amplifies their appeal
item.description: Two new reports are a must-read of energy-starved governments

item.title: How global can the yuan get?
item.description: The anxiety of war has been good for the yuan

item.title: Xi Jinping wants a powerful currency. America’s war has helped
item.description: More countries are starting to use China’s payment infrastructure

item.title: Chinamaxxing is starting to catch on, in China
item.description: It’s a boon for the tourist trade

item.title: What do the geopolitical successes of Asim Munir mean for Pakistan?
item.description: Despite some breathless optimism, the military strongman is unlikely to deliver deeper change

item.title: Vietnam and South Korea Agree to Boost Cooperation on Supply Chains, Nuclear Energy
item.description: Amid the current conflict in the Middle East, President Lee Jae Myung said that "the need for cooperation between our nations has grown even greater."

item.title: How China and Russia Can Exploit the Iran War
item.description: And how America can avoid playing into their hands.

item.title: A Gaza for Gazans
item.description: Palestinians must lead the rebuilding of the strip.

item.title: Thailand’s Fuel Smuggling Problem Surges Into View
item.description: The Hormuz oil crisis has exacerbated a long-standing problem, heaping pressure on Prime Minister Anutin's government to address the ongoing “national oil heist.”

item.title: Indonesian Finance Minister Raises Possibility of Imposing Toll on Strait of Malacca
item.description: Whether or not it was serious, Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa's suggestion is a sign of how far the norm of freedom of peaceful navigation has been eroded by the Iran war.

item.title: The Australian Labor Party’s Foreign Policy No Longer Matches the World’s Realities
item.description: The party's platform clings to the U.S. alliance while ignoring its own commitment to self-reliance and the opportunities of the Asia-Pacific.

item.title: Philippine Legislators Set to Vote on Impeachment Complaint Against Vice President
item.description: As in 2025, Sara Duterte is accused of betraying public trust, misusing government funds, and plotting to assassinate the First Family.

item.title: The Security Imperative of India’s Clean Energy Transition
item.description: India’s energy security hinges on breaking its dependence on fossil fuels.

item.title: Crypto-miners are quietly colonising other people’s computers
item.description: Hijacking processing power cuts costs

item.title: Donald Trump’s bold new deportation machine
item.description: What lies behind the administration’s grubby deals with African countries
